# CMTIMERANGE_IS_INDEFINITE(_:)

**Function**

Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a time range is indefinite.

## Declaration

```swift
func CMTIMERANGE_IS_INDEFINITE(_ range: CMTimeRange) -> Bool
```

## Parameters

- **range**: A time range.

## Return Value

[doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/true] if `range` is indefinite; otherwise, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Swift/false].

## Inspecting Time Ranges

- **CMTIMERANGE_IS_EMPTY(_:)**: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a time range has a duration of zero.
- **CMTIMERANGE_IS_INVALID(_:)**: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a time range is invalid.
- **CMTIMERANGE_IS_VALID(_:)**: Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a time range is valid.
- **CMTimeRangeGetEnd(_:)**: Returns a time value that represents the end of a time range.
- **CMTimeRangeGetIntersection(_:otherRange:)**: Returns a new time range with the time elements that are common between the input.
- **CMTimeRangeGetUnion(_:otherRange:)**: Returns a new time range with the time elements of the input.
